lines.target: If you go to the Tower of London, you will see six ravens.
lines.base: Si va usted a la Torre de Londres, verá usted seis cuervos.

lines.target: These birds — or rather their ancestors — have been there since the eleventh century.
lines.base: Estos pájaros — o más bien sus antepasados — han estado allí desde el siglo once.

lines.target: But, today, they are there for a very important reason.
lines.base: Pero, hoy día están allí por una razón muy importante.

lines.target: According to tradition, the British Empire will remain only as long as there are ravens in the Tower.
lines.base: Según la tradición, el Imperio Británico permanecerá solo mientras haya cuervos en la Torre.

lines.target: No one knows the reason for this legend, but there are many suggestions.
lines.base: Nadie sabe la razón de esta leyenda, pero hay muchas hipótesis (sugerencias).

lines.target: One is that a gang of thieves broke into the Tower while the sentries were asleep,
lines.base: Una es que una banda de ladrones entró en la Torre mientras los centinelas estaban dormidos,

lines.target: but the ravens made so much noise that the sentries woke up and were able to kill the thieves.
lines.base: pero los cuervos hicieron tanto ruido que los centinelas se despertaron y pudieron matar a los ladrones.

lines.target: Whatever the reason, the birds are fed every day and receive a State pension.
lines.base: Sea cual sea la razón, los pájaros son alimentados diariamente y reciben una pensión del estado.

lines.target: Scotland and Ireland, too, are full of legend and tradition.
lines.base: Escocia e Irlanda, también, están llenas de leyendas y tradiciones.

lines.target: In Scotland, Christmas is not a big feast. For the Scots, New Year's Day is more important.
lines.base: En Escocia, la Navidad no es una gran fiesta. Para los escoceses el día de Año Nuevo es más importante.

lines.target: This is called "Hogmanay". At midnight, a tall dark man must cross the threshold of your house
lines.base: Este se llaman Hogmanay. A medianoche, un hombre alto y moreno debe cruzar el umbral de la casa

lines.target: carrying a lump of coal, a piece of bread and a bottle of whisky.
lines.base: llevando un trozo de carbón, un pedazo de pan y una botella de whisky.

lines.target: These items symbolise warmth, food and drink for the coming year.
lines.base: Estos artículos simbolizan el calor, la comida y la bebida para el año venidero.

gap_candidates.target: If you go to the Tower of London, you will see six ravens.
According to tradition, the British Empire will remain only as long as there are ravens in the Tower.
No one knows the reason for this legend, but there are many suggestions.
One is that a gang of thieves broke into the Tower while the sentries were asleep,
but the ravens made so much noise that the sentries woke up and were able to kill the thieves.
Whatever the reason, the birds are fed every day and receive a State pension.
Scotland and Ireland, too, are full of legend and tradition.
In Scotland, Christmas is not a big feast. For the Scots, New Year's Day is more important.
This is called "Hogmanay". At midnight, a tall dark man must cross the threshold of your house
carrying a lump of coal, a piece of bread and a bottle of whisky.
